sections.paragraph:
(with the adj. termination ־ָן, like נְחֻשְׁתָּן brazen, from נְחשֶׁת, עֲקַלָּתֹון from עֲקַלָּה), prop. an (animal), wreathed, twisted in folds.

(1) a serpent of a larger kind, Job 3:8 (as to this place see the root עוּר Pilel); Isa 27:1 (where it is the symbol of the hostile kingdom of Babylon).

(2) specially, a crocodile, Job 40:25 , seq.

(3) any very large aquatic creature, Psa 104:26 used for a fierce enemy, Psa 74:14 comp. תַּנִּין Isa 51:9 Eze 29:3 32:2 , Eze 32:3. Bochart, Hieroz. P. ii. lib. v. cap. 16-18.
